benefits.title: Programme teams can evidence and claim their graduate futures value more confidently
benefits.statement: Programme teams can make stronger, more nuanced and evidence-informed claims about the futures their programme makes possible for students, including distinctive excellence, student pathways, outcomes and value.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, students, applicants, recruitment teams and King's Edge
benefits.benefitType: programme-level graduate futures evidence and articulation
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme-level narratives claim graduate futures value with confidence, nuance and evidence, while remaining context-sensitive, academically owned and appropriately caveated. These local stories are strong enough to be used by programme teams and to feed King’s-level graduate premium synthesis through 2.4.1.
evidenceOfSuccess: Number and percentage of agreed programme groups with a completed Programme Graduate Futures Profile, programme reflection or equivalent story artefact.
Number and percentage of completed profiles or story artefacts confirmed by the relevant programme team as accurate, usable and appropriately caveated.
Number of evidence-informed programme stories or claims used in programme review, recruitment, open days, student-facing contexts or applicant-facing contexts.
Number of programme-level story inputs passed forward for King’s-level graduate premium synthesis through 2.4.1.

enabledBy.outputTitle: First working graduate futures evidence pack

benefits.title: Programme teams and faculties understand graduate futures enhancement opportunities better
benefits.statement: Programme teams, departments and faculties can see where existing strengths should be amplified and where curriculum, skills, opportunity, support or articulation work could most strengthen students' futures.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, Careers and Employability Service, King's Edge and students
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme and faculty conversations use graduate futures evidence to identify strengths, questions, support needs and focused opportunities for enhancement without treating the evidence as a deficit report, ranking or judgement on programme quality. These local insights are clear enough to support action and to feed King’s-level pattern-finding through 2.4.1.
evidenceOfSuccess: Number and percentage of agreed programme groups completing a guided conversation or enabled self-review.
Number and percentage of completed conversations or reviews producing at least one agreed question, focus area, support need or local action.
Number and percentage of local actions or focus areas routed into programme, department or faculty planning.
Number of faculty-level themes, recurring support needs or enhancement opportunities passed forward for King’s-level synthesis through 2.4.1.

enabledBy.outputTitle: Examples of programme insight and story
Recommendations for related King's Edge work

measures.title: Programme graduate futures stories completed, agreed and used
measures.questionAnswered: How many agreed programme groups are producing credible, evidence-linked graduate futures claims that programme teams can use?
measures.measure: Count and percentage of agreed programme groups with a completed Programme Graduate Futures Profile, reflection or equivalent story artefact; count and percentage of those artefacts confirmed by the relevant programme team as accurate, usable and appropriately caveated; count of evidence-informed programme stories or claims used in review, recruitment, open days, student-facing or applicant-facing contexts; count of programme-level story inputs passed forward for 2.4.1.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, with support from Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: Termly during supported conversations, with annual synthesis
measures.baseline: 0 agreed programme groups have completed a graduate futures profile, reflection or equivalent story artefact through this model.
measures.target: Target to be set once the agreed programme-group denominator is confirmed. Minimum expected evidence: each agreed programme group completes a profile, reflection or equivalent story artefact; completed artefacts are programme-confirmed before external or student-facing use; story inputs suitable for 2.4.1 are logged.

measures.title: Programme and faculty enhancement insights generated and routed
measures.questionAnswered: How many agreed programme groups and faculties are generating actionable graduate futures enhancement insight?
measures.measure: Count and percentage of agreed programme groups completing a guided conversation or enabled self-review; count and percentage of completed conversations or reviews producing at least one agreed question, focus area, support need or local action; count and percentage of local actions or focus areas routed into programme, department or faculty planning; count of faculty-level themes, recurring support needs or enhancement opportunities passed forward for 2.4.1.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, faculty education leads, Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: Termly during supported conversations, with annual synthesis
measures.baseline: 0 agreed programme groups have completed a guided conversation or enabled self-review through this model.
measures.target: Target to be set once the agreed programme-group denominator is confirmed. Minimum expected evidence: each agreed programme group completes a guided conversation or enabled self-review; completed reviews produce at least one agreed question, focus area, support need or local action; faculty-level themes suitable for 2.4.1 are logged.
